title: SIREH workshop connects students with Armenian heritage at the Shamiram archaeological site
content:
On 23 June 2026, European Heritage Hub partner SIREH organised a one-day field workshop at the Shamiram archaeological site in Armenia’s Aragatsotn region, within the framework of the Hub. The workshop brought six students into the heart of a working excavation, offering them a first-hand encounter with archaeological research as it actually unfolds.

The Shamiram site is one of the largest and most complex fortified settlements in central Armenia, defined by at least six lines of walls distributed across a broad rocky plateau. Excavations are conducted by the Armenian-Italian Archaeological Mission, directed by archaeologist Roberto Dan (ISMEO) together with Varduhi Melikyan (Institute of Archaeology and Ethnography NAS RA). Recent campaigns have brought to light a monumental fortification system, walls, towers, and buttresses, attributed to the late phase of the Kingdom of Urartu, as well as evidence of the long life of the settlement, from the Iron Age through the Orontid and Artaxiad periods and into medieval times.

creator: Liv Martin

title: Breathing new life into sacred spaces: Exploring Rotterdam’s innovative approach to religious heritage
content:
From historic churches and community storytelling spaces to innovative museum projects and grassroots cultural initiatives, Rotterdam welcomed 20 participants of the European Heritage Hub’s Peer-learning Visit from 18-20 June for three days of exchange, discovery and reflection. 

Hosted by the Municipality of Rotterdam and organised in collaboration with Hub partner Eurocities, the visit brought together city representatives, heritage professionals, policymakers and cultural practitioners from across Europe to explore how heritage can strengthen social cohesion, support urban transformation and foster community participation.

The programme opened with a welcome from Martijn van der Mark, Senior Policy Advisor for the Creative Industries and Media at the City of Rotterdam, and Anouk Estourgie, Cultural Historian and programme maker, Cultuur & Vliegwerk, alongside members of the European Heritage Hub team. Tanja Mlaker, Director of Cultuur Eindhoven, served as the Peer-learning Visit expert and facilitator.
